_comment_doc: Sunfish main-branch ruleset, modeled with GitHub's Rulesets API (POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets). This is the recommended modern replacement for the legacy branch-protection rule in branch-protection-main.json. See infra/github/README.md for when to use which. The leading _comment_* keys are stripped by apply-main-ruleset.sh before POST/PATCH (the GitHub API rejects unknown top-level fields).
_comment_paths_ignore_hazard: GitHub Rulesets, like legacy branch protection, will leave a required check in 'Pending' if the workflow is skipped (paths/paths-ignore filter, branches filter, commit-message skip, etc.). The required-check list below was trimmed from the original 7 to 4 always-on checks specifically to avoid this hazard — see _comment_required_checks_choice on the parameters object below for the full rationale and re-inclusion path. Background documented in README.md §'Paths-ignore hazard'.
name: main-protection

parameters._comment_required_checks_choice: Trimmed from the original 7-check list to only the always-on checks (10/10 appearances on recent PRs). The 4 global-ux-gate checks (CSS logical-properties audit, Locale completeness check, A11y Storybook test-runner, Global-UX Gate aggregate) are gated by paths: filters in global-ux-gate.yml and skip on docs/security/wolf/etc. PRs — requiring them under strict mode would permanently block those PRs. Build & Test similarly skips via paths-ignore in ci.yml. Re-add to required-checks list once those workflows are restructured to fire on every PR (or move the relevant gates into ci.yml).
